title: Ube Colored Filipino Toor Dal Poke
description: A unique and refreshing Filipino dish featuring Ube-colored toor dal, perfect for a festive and earthy twist on a traditional raw-friendly poke bowl.
instructions: Rinse the toor dal and soak it in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the toor dal, then transfer it to a blender or food processor.
Add 100g Ube halaya, 20g cilantro, 20g mint leaves, 60ml lime juice, 2g salt, 1g black pepper, 20g ginger, and 20g chili peppers to the blender.
Blend the mixture until it's smooth and well combined.
Heat 20g coconut oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the blended mixture to the pan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 300ml water to the pan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Stir in 100g papaya and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the toor dal mixture to a large bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, refrigerate the toor dal mixture for at least 2 hours to allow it to chill and set.
Just before serving, garnish the toor dal with additional cilantro and mint leaves, if desired.
